name: test-coverage-analyzer
description: Analyzes test coverage gaps and suggests test cases for untested code paths. Use when user requests test improvements, coverage analysis, or wants to increase test coverage.
qualityReason: Clear purpose, overlaps slightly with advisor
capabilities: Detects testing frameworks (Jest, pytest, Go test, JUnit, Mocha, Vitest)
Parses coverage reports in JSON, lcov.info, .coverage, and coverage.out formats
Identifies files and functions with low coverage
Suggests specific test cases for uncovered code paths
Generates test file stubs for JavaScript, Python, and Go
Calculates coverage metrics and prioritizes critical gaps
author: CrazyDubya
securitySummary: This skill is a legitimate test coverage analyzer with no malicious behavior. The bash script parses standard coverage file formats using common CLI tools. All static findings are false positives: external commands (jq, grep, awk) are standard development tools, filesystem access is read-only, and the critical heuristic finding misinterprets benign coverage parsing as dangerous behavior. No network calls, no credential access, no data exfiltration.
valueStatement: Identifying untested code paths manually is time consuming. This skill parses coverage reports to find gaps and suggests specific test cases to improve code quality.
limitations: Does not execute tests or modify source code directly
Requires existing coverage reports to analyze
Cannot generate tests for complex dependencies without user guidance
Limited to supported coverage formats (JSON, lcov, Python, Go)

useCases.title: Improve coverage metrics
useCases.description: Identify low-coverage files and prioritize test writing efforts based on business impact

useCases.title: Write missing tests
useCases.description: Get specific suggestions for untested code paths with example test cases and assertions

useCases.title: Verify test adequacy
useCases.description: Review coverage gaps before merging code to ensure critical paths are tested

promptTemplates.title: Basic coverage check
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze test coverage for this project. Find coverage reports and identify files with the lowest coverage. List the top 5 priorities for adding tests.

promptTemplates.title: Generate test cases
promptTemplates.prompt: Find coverage gaps in src/utils/validator.js. What lines are untested? Suggest 3 specific test cases to cover the missing paths.

promptTemplates.title: Framework detection
promptTemplates.prompt: What testing framework does this project use? Do they have coverage configured? Show me the current coverage summary.

promptTemplates.title: Full coverage plan
promptTemplates.prompt: Run a complete coverage analysis. List all files under 60% coverage with specific line numbers and suggested test cases for each gap. Generate test file stubs where helpful.

bestPractices: Focus on critical paths first: authentication, payments, and data validation
Test behavior over implementation details for maintainable tests
Set minimum coverage thresholds (e.g., 80%) and track progress over time
antiPatterns: Writing tests only to increase coverage numbers without validating behavior
Ignoring edge cases and error handling paths in tests
Testing private methods or implementation details instead of public APIs

faq.question: What coverage formats are supported?
faq.answer: Jest coverage-summary.json, lcov.info, Python .coverage, and Go coverage.out formats are supported.

faq.question: What is a good coverage target?
faq.answer: 80% is a common target, but prioritize business-critical code over achieving 100% coverage.

faq.question: Does this modify my code?
faq.answer: No. This skill only reads coverage data and suggests tests. It does not modify source or test files.

faq.question: Is my code data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. All analysis happens locally. Code is only read to identify gaps, never transmitted externally.

faq.question: Why no coverage report found?
faq.answer: Run your test suite with coverage: npm test -- --coverage, pytest --cov, or go test -cover.

faq.question: How is this different from coverage tools?
faq.answer: Coverage tools show what is covered. This skill explains what is NOT covered and suggests specific tests.
